forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Patchy fog after 2am. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 71. Southwest wind around 8 mph.

forecast.name: Friday
forecast.shortForecast: Patchy Fog then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast.detailedForecast: Patchy fog before 7am, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly sunny, with a high near 89. Northwest wind around 7 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.

weather_report:
As night envelops Boston, a gentle hush falls over the city, interrupted only by the distant hum of urban life. The air is heavy with warmth, holding a promise of moisture as patchy fog tiptoes into existence after the clock strikes two. The low temperature caresses the skin at around seventy-one degrees, while a soft southwest breeze whispers through the darkness. It’s a moment not unkind, though the view may be obscured; familiarity with the terrain brings comfort even as night bathes it in shadows.

As dawn approaches tomorrow, expect the fog to fade into a canvas painted with daylight, revealing the vibrancy of summer. The day will unfurl itself under mostly sunny skies, bearing the heat of a high near eighty-nine. The chance of thunderstorms later introduces a thrill, a possibility of nature’s spectacle, though not an overwhelming certainty, as only a gentle thirty percent stands on the horizon. Slip into comfortable attire—perhaps a loose-fitting shirt and lightweight shorts, lest the warmth become insistent. It is a day well-suited for leisurely endeavors: a midday stroll along the Charles, or perhaps a quiet moment with a book at a café, as clouds begin to gather, hinting at the excitement that may come later.

The atmosphere dances with anticipation, urging one to enjoy the daylight while it lasts, knowing that summer storms can shift the day’s energy in an instant.
